# Agents (automations, bots, and AI assistants)

Purpose
- Define what agents are allowed to do in this repository, how they should behave, and how maintainers should operate with them.
- Provide clear safety, security, and review guidance so automation improves velocity without introducing risk.

Agent types
- Copilot coding agent: automated assistant that can propose branches, commits, and PRs.
- Dependency/maintenance bots: Dependabot, Renovate, etc.
- CI/Quality bots: linters, formatters, test runners that report status.
- Triage/labeling bots: auto-label PRs/issues, apply templates.
- Custom agents: project-specific automation (e.g., release automation).

Onboarding & ownership
- Every agent must have a documented owner (person or team) responsible for:
  - Reviewing its behavior
  - Responding to incidents
  - Maintaining configuration
- Document owner as "Agent Owner: @username" in the agent's configuration or this file.

Naming and branching conventions
- Branches created by agents must use a predictable prefix:
  - copilot/* for Copilot-generated branches
  - deps/* for dependency updates
  - ci/* for automated CI fixes
- Branches must include a short reason in the name, e.g., copilot/add-funding-config

Prompting & behavior guidelines
- Keep prompts minimal and deterministic; prefer templates stored in the repo.
- Agents must include a short explanation of their changes in each commit and PR body.
- Agents must not make breaking changes without an explicit human review item in the PR.

Pull requests and review rules
- Any PR created by an agent must:
  - Use a clear title and description that includes "Generated by <agent-name>"
  - Add a human reviewer (or team) and at least one maintainer as an assignee
  - Not be merged automatically unless the owner explicitly enables auto-merge for that agent and the PR passes CI and approvals
- For Copilot: set PR label "agent/generated" and "needs-review" automatically.

Permissions & security
- Use least-privilege: agents should have the minimal GitHub app or token scopes required.
- Do not store secrets in agent prompts or committed files.
- Any step that requires elevated permissions (deploy, publish) must require manual approval.
- Agents must log actions and provide traceable audit metadata in PR descriptions and commit messages.

Secrets handling
- Never embed secrets in commits or PRs.
- Use repository secrets and environment secrets in GitHub Actions.
- Agent prompts stored in the repo must not include sensitive data.

Human-in-the-loop & escalation
- Agents must explicitly request human review when:
  - Making API changes, infra changes, or anything labeled "security" or "breaking change"
  - Changing deployment or billing config
- Maintain an incident channel (e.g., Slack or GitHub Discussions) and contact info for each agent owner.

Testing & CI
- Agent-created PRs must run full CI (lint, unit tests) before merging.
- If PRs introduce new tests, they must pass on the agent branch before review.

Transparency & documentation
- Agents should add a short changelog entry or PR note summarizing what was changed and why.
- Keep agent configuration and prompts under .github/agents or .github/agents/*.

Metrics & monitoring
- Track:
  - Number of agent-created PRs
  - Merge rate and revert rate
  - Test failures caused by agent PRs
- Review metrics monthly to tune agent behavior.

Example prompts (templates)
- copilot/pr_description_template.md — include a fixed PR description template that the agent must fill.
- Example: "This PR was generated by Copilot to add FUNDING support. Summary: <one-line>. Tests: <status>. Manual steps: <if any>."

Revision & review cadence
- Review agent permissions and configurations quarterly.
- Keep a record of agent changes in this file with date + owner + change summary.
